query: Latest developments relevant to narada operations
raw_intelligence:
**Narada Power Source (Zhejiang Narada Power Co., Ltd.) announced on December 11, 2025, that its controlling shareholders—Hangzhou Narada Power Source Co., Ltd., Shanghai Yidu Industrial Co., Ltd., and Shanghai Narada Group Co., Ltd.—plan to change the company's control rights and sell equity in its recycled lead segment, potentially altering the controlling shareholder and actual controller.[1]**

This follows the 2025 half-year report showing Zhou Qingzhi and associates holding a 10.52% stake via Hangzhou Narada, amid financial pressures: 2024 full-year revenue of 7.984 billion yuan with a 1.497 billion yuan net loss, and first three quarters of 2025 revenue at 5.911 billion yuan (down 24.80% YoY) with a 220 million yuan net loss.[1]

**Key operational shifts include voluntary production cuts in recycled lead post-strategic transformation, causing a 2 billion yuan YoY revenue drop in that segment, offset by strong growth in industrial energy storage and data center businesses.[1]**

As of October 29, 2025, undelivered orders total ~8.9 billion yuan, with ~5.5 billion yuan in utility-scale energy storage (4 billion yuan domestic, 1.5 billion yuan overseas from Australia, Europe, UK).[1]

**Separately, Anhui Narada Huatuo New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. (a Narada affiliate) signed deals with Sunrise New Energy: a December 2025 order for 246 tons of graphite anode materials (annualizing to 3,000 tons from 2026, valued at ~$11 million) for high-rate discharge applications in UAVs, energy storage, and batteries; this targets sodium-ion/lithium-ion growth in telecom backup, power tools, and beyond.[3][4]**

No other major operational updates appear in recent data; unrelated results (e.g., Indian rail, temples) do not pertain to Narada entities. Financials indicate transformation risks but order backlog supports energy storage pivot.[1]
